<!-- cursoride Session ec97028c-196e-4bfe-becb-1a24a55fe833 (2026-04-15 13:04:36Z) -->

_**User**_

Create search screen where header is same as @src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx but instead of notification icon show a small circular profile icon. Show search bar just below header use surface_container_low background, 12px radius with Magnifier icon left, placeholder: "Search movies, actors, directors..." and Focused state: subtle outline_variant ghost border at 15% opacity.

Below search bar show Horizontally scrollable like Same chip style as Home. Tapping pre-populates the search bar and immediately triggers a search.

Below scrollable chips show Section title "Recent Searches" (headline-md) with "CLEAR ALL" right-aligned (primary_container, title-sm uppercase) Show Up to 5 entries. These should be save using asynstorage. This recent serach section only visible when search bar is empty.

Below this show Trending Now section. Use same API used to show trending now on @src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx . This section will use same @src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx and will show in grid contains two column only.

When user start search Call GET /search/movie?query={q} API, It will give search results. Response parameter would be results[].id, title, poster_path, vote_average, release_date, genre_ids. Use 400 ms debouncing logic in search. Search results will be visible in 2 column grid format.

Attaching screenshot for reference.

'./types';\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nfunction normalizeError(err: unknown): string {\\\\\\\\n  return err instanceof ApiClientError ? err.message : 'Something went wrong';\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport function usePaginatedMovieList(\\\\\\\\n  resetKey: unknown,\\\\\\\\n  fetchPage: (page: number) =\u003e Promise\u003cPaginatedResponse\u003cMovie\u003e\u003e,\\\\\\\\n): UsePaginatedMoviesResult {\\\\\\\\n  const [movies, setMovies] = useState\u003cMovie[]\u003e([]);\\\\\\\\n  const [page, setPage] = useState(0);\\\\\\\\n  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\\\\\\\\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\\\\\\\\n  const [loadingMore, setLoadingMore] = useState(false);\\\\\\\\n  const [error, setError] = useState\u003cstring | null\u003e(null);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const fetchPageRef = useRef(fetchPage);\\\\\\\\n  fetchPageRef.current = fetchPage;\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const pageRef = useRef(0);\\\\\\\\n  const totalPagesRef = useRef(1);\\\\\\\\n  const loadingMoreRef = useRef(false);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n    pageRef.current = page;\\\\\\\\n  }, [page]);\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n    totalPagesRef.current = totalPages;\\\\\\\\n  }, [totalPages]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  useEffect(() =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n    let cancelled = false;\\\\\\\\n    (async () =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n      setLoading(true);\\\\\\\\n      setError(null);\\\\\\\\n      try {\\\\\\\\n        const res = await fetchPageRef.current(1);\\\\\\\\n        if (cancelled) {\\\\\\\\n          return;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        setMovies(res.results);\\\\\\\\n        const newPage = 1;\\\\\\\\n        setPage(newPage);\\\\\\\\n        pageRef.current = newPage;\\\\\\\\n        setTotalPages(res.total_pages);\\\\\\\\n        totalPagesRef.current = res.total_pages;\\\\\\\\n      } catch (err) {\\\\\\\\n        if (cancelled) {\\\\\\\\n          return;\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n        setMovies([]);\\\\\\\\n        setPage(0);\\\\\\\\n        pageRef.current = 0;\\\\\\\\n        setError(normalizeError(err));\\\\\\\\n      } finally {\\\\\\\\n        if (!cancelled) {\\\\\\\\n          setLoading(false);\\\\\\\\n        }\\\\\\\\n      }\\\\\\\\n    })();\\\\\\\\n    return () =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n      cancelled = true;\\\\\\\\n    };\\\\\\\\n  }, [resetKey]);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  const appendNextPage = useCallback(async (): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e =\u003e {\\\\\\\\n    if (loadingMoreRef.current) {\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n    const currentPage = pageRef.current;\\\\\\\\n    const tp = totalPagesRef.current;\\\\\\\\n    if (currentPage === 0 || currentPage \u003e= tp) {\\\\\\\\n      return;\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n    loadingMoreRef.current = true;\\\\\\\\n    setLoadingMore(true);\\\\\\\\n    setError(null);\\\\\\\\n    try {\\\\\\\\n      const res = await fetchPageRef.current(currentPage + 1);\\\\\\\\n      setMovies(prev =\u003e [...prev, ...res.results]);\\\\\\\\n      const nextPage = currentPage + 1;\\\\\\\\n      setPage(nextPage);\\\\\\\\n      pageRef.current = nextPage;\\\\\\\\n      setTotalPages(res.total_pages);\\\\\\\\n      totalPagesRef.current = res.total_pages;\\\\\\\\n    } catch (err) {\\\\\\\\n      setError(normalizeError(err));\\\\\\\\n    } finally {\\\\\\\\n      loadingMoreRef.current = false;\\\\\\\\n      setLoadingMore(false);\\\\\\\\n    }\\\\\\\\n  }, []);\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n  return {\\\\\\\\n    movies,\\\\\\\\n    page,\\\\\\\\n    totalPages,\\\\\\\\n    loading,\\\\\\\\n    loadingMore,\\\\\\\\n    error,\\\\\\\\n    appendNextPage,\\\\\\\\n  };\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport function usePaginatedTrending(): UsePaginatedMoviesResult {\\\\\\\\n  const fetchPage = useCallback((p: number) =\u003e getTrending(p), []);\\\\\\\\n  return usePaginatedMovieList('trending', fetchPage);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport function usePaginatedTopRated(): fetchPage);\\\\\\\\n}\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nexport function useDiscoverMovies(\\\\\\\\n  withGenresId: number | null,\\\\\\\\n): UsePaginatedMoviesResult {\\\\\\\\n  const fetchPage = useCallback(\\\\\\\\n    (p: number) =\u003e discoverMovies(p, withGenresId ?? undefined),\\\\\\\\n    [withGenresId],\\\\\\\\n  );\\\\\\\\n  return usePaginatedMovieList(withGenresId, Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a plan for the SearchScreen redesign.

4. `GenreChipStrip` component exists and can be reused
5. `ContentCard` component exists and can be reused


6. The `usePaginatedTrending` hook is available for the Trending Now section, and the `searchMovies` API already handles the `/search/movie` endpoint

For the rewrite, I need to update SearchScreen.tsx with the design specs - the header and search bar are mostly in place, but I'll need to add the AsyncStorage-backed Recent Searches section and ensure all styling matches the specifications.

I also need to create a new `useRecentSearches` hook to manage the recent searches with AsyncStorage, and looking at the screenshots, the Trending Now section uses a special layout where the first card spans full width and the rest are in a 2-column grid. The search results grid also needs to change from 3 columns to 2 columns, and I should display the result count text. The user wants a 2-column grid of ContentCards for Trending Now, but the screenshot shows a featured card displayed full-width at the top before the grid starts. Let me reconcile this—it seems like the first item gets special treatment as a featured card, then the remaining items flow into the 2-column layout below it. I'm going back and forth on whether the featured card is part of the requirements or just shown in the screenshot as reference. The user's text explicitly says to use ContentCard in a 2-column grid, so I'll stick with that interpretation and build just the grid layout without the featured card for now.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation plan: I need to create a custom hook for managing recent searches with AsyncStorage, then refactor the SearchScreen to include a Recent Searches section that appears when the search bar is empty, add the Trending Now section with a 2-column ContentCard grid, and adjust the search results layout accordingly.

Looking at the screenshot more carefully, the Trending Now section appears to have a featured card displayed prominently at the top, followed by the 2-column grid below it—so I should structure it that way rather than treating all cards uniformly. I'm checking ContentCard's capabilities—it has a style prop for width control, so I could make the first item full-width and others half-width. But ContentCard lacks the featured badge and duration display that the screenshot shows. I'm going to stick with the explicit instruction to use ContentCard in a simple 2-column grid for all items, even though the screenshot suggests a featured card design. I need to create a new hook for recent searches backed by AsyncStorage and do a major rewrite of SearchScreen to include all the new sections. The existing `useSearch` and `usePaginatedTrending` hooks already handle debouncing and fetching, and `GenreChipStrip` and `ContentCard` components are ready to use. Let me review the screenshots once more to make sure I'm matching the exact layout and details the user wants. Looking at the screenshot more carefully, the Trending Now section actually starts with a featured card that has the "FEATURED" badge and full-width poster layout—similar to the HomeScreen's hero card—rather than a standard ContentCard. So I'll structure it with that featured card at the top using the first trending movie, then follow with the 2-column grid for the rest. I'm settling on the simplest approach—a 2-column grid of ContentCard components for all trending movies, and if the user wants a featured card treatment later, we can add that. Looking at the screenshots again, the recent searches use clock icons with titles, and the search results show a 2-column grid with posters, titles, years, and rating badges, which ContentCard already handles well.</details></think>

  "plan": "# Redesign Search Screen\n\n## Current State\n\n[&#96;src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx) already exists and is wired into the tab navigator. It has:\n- Correct header (brand + profile icon)\n- Search bar with &#96;surface_container_low&#96; background and 12px radius\n- &#96;GenreChipStrip&#96; for genre chips\n- 3-column search results grid\n\n**Missing:** Recent Searches (AsyncStorage), Trending Now section, 2-column layout, result count text.\n\n## Architecture\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    SearchScreen --\u003e useSearch[\"useSearch (existing)\"]\n    SearchScreen --\u003e useRecentSearches[\"useRecentSearches (new)\"]\n    SearchScreen --\u003e usePaginatedTrending[\"usePaginatedTrending (existing)\"]\n    SearchScreen --\u003e GenreChipStrip[\"GenreChipStrip (existing)\"]\n    SearchScreen --\u003e ContentCard[\"ContentCard (existing)\"]\n    useSearch --\u003e searchMovies[\"searchMovies API (existing)\"]\n    usePaginatedTrending --\u003e getTrending[\"getTrending API (existing)\"]\n    useRecentSearches --\u003e AsyncStorage\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Files to Create\n\n### 1. &#96;src/hooks/useRecentSearches.ts&#96;\n\nNew hook to manage recent searches via &#96;@react-native-async-storage/async-storage&#96; (already in &#96;package.json&#96;).\n\n- **State:** &#96;searches: string[]&#96; (max 5 entries)\n- **&#96;addSearch(term: string)&#96;** -- prepend to list, deduplicate, cap at 5, persist\n- **&#96;clearAll()&#96;** -- clear list and remove from storage\n- **Load on mount** from AsyncStorage key &#96;@streamlist/recent_searches&#96;\n- Return &#96;{searches, addSearch, clearAll}&#96;\n\n### 2. Modify &#96;src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx&#96;\n\nMajor rewrite structured as a single &#96;ScrollView&#96; (or &#96;FlatList&#96; with &#96;ListHeaderComponent&#96;) that conditionally shows sections based on search state.\n\n**Layout when search bar is empty (default state):**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[Header: brand + profile icon]\n[Search bar]\n[Genre chip strip]\n[Recent Searches section]     \u003c-- NEW\n[Trending Now 2-col grid]     \u003c-- NEW\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Layout when search has results:**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[Header: brand + profile icon]\n[Search bar]\n[\"{count} results for '{query}'\" text]\n[2-column grid of search results]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Specific changes:**\n\n- **Grid columns:** Change &#96;NUM_COLUMNS&#96; and results exist\n- **Recent Searches section** (only visible when &#96;query&#96; is empty):\n  - \"Recent Searches\" title (&#96;headline_md&#96;, &#96;on_surface&#96;)\n  - \"CLEAR ALL\" right-aligned (&#96;primary_container&#96;, &#96;title_sm&#96;, uppercase)\n  - Up to 5 rows, each with a clock icon (&#96;Ionicons time-outline&#96;) and the search term text\n  - Tapping a recent search entry populates the search bar and triggers search\n- **Trending Now section** (only visible when &#96;query&#96;
